title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school has asked you to write a report on students' opinions about the school canteen, based on a survey. Include findings about students' satisfaction with the current food options, their preferences for improvement, and recommendations for making the canteen healthier and more appealing to students.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Student Satisfaction with Maple Grove School Canteen

Introduction
This report presents the findings of a survey completed by 175 students regarding their views on the school canteen's current food provision and their suggestions for improvement.

Current Satisfaction Levels
Overall satisfaction with the canteen was low. Only 22% of respondents rated the food quality as good or excellent, and fewer than a third felt that there were sufficient healthy options available. The most frequent complaints concerned the limited variety of hot meals and the excessive use of heavily processed ingredients.

Student Preferences
Students expressed a strong preference for freshly prepared meals with clearly displayed nutritional information. Many also requested a greater range of vegetarian and vegan options, as well as more diverse international cuisines. A significant number of respondents indicated they would use the canteen more regularly if portion sizes were increased without a corresponding rise in price.

Recommendations
The school should engage a nutritionist to review and redesign the weekly menu. Introducing a rotating 'world cuisine' theme each week and involving students in recipe selection would increase engagement. A trial of self-service salad and hot food bars is also strongly recommended as a cost-effective way to increase variety.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a food and travel website:

'Restaurant and café reviews wanted! Tell us about a local restaurant or café you have visited recently. Describe the food, the atmosphere, the service, and the value for money — and let us know if you would go back.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
The Olive Branch Café — A Hidden Gem Worth Discovering

Tucked away in a quiet side street near the town centre, The Olive Branch is the kind of place you wish you had discovered years earlier. Having visited twice in recent weeks, I can say with confidence that it has become one of my favourite spots in the city.

The menu is Mediterranean-inspired with a strong focus on seasonal, locally sourced ingredients. I tried the halloumi and roasted vegetable flatbread on my first visit and the butternut squash soup with homemade sourdough on my second — both were outstanding. The pastries and cakes, displayed invitingly on the counter, are also well worth a visit in themselves.

The atmosphere is wonderfully relaxed, with warm lighting, mismatched wooden furniture, and a genuinely welcoming feel. Service was attentive and friendly on both occasions without being intrusive.

In terms of value, prices are very reasonable given the quality — a main meal and drink costs around £12, which seems entirely fair.

I would wholeheartedly recommend The Olive Branch to anyone looking for a comfortable, unpretentious café serving genuinely excellent food.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Kitchen confidence: Write an article about the benefits of learning to cook. Why is cooking an important life skill? What advice would you give to someone who wants to start cooking for themselves?'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Why Everyone Should Know How to Cook

In an age of meal delivery apps and ready-made supermarket meals, learning to cook can feel unnecessary. Yet cooking from scratch remains one of the most valuable skills any person can develop — and the reasons go far beyond simply saving money.

Cooking empowers you to take control of what you eat. When you prepare food yourself, you know precisely what ingredients you are consuming, which makes it far easier to maintain a balanced, nutritious diet. It also provides an outlet for creativity and a genuine source of satisfaction — few things feel as rewarding as serving a meal you made entirely yourself.

Beyond the practical benefits, cooking together is a powerful way to connect with others. Many of the most meaningful conversations happen around food.

For anyone wanting to start, my advice is simple: begin with three or four reliable, straightforward recipes and practise them until you feel confident. Build gradually, use good quality ingredients, and do not be discouraged by early mistakes. Every experienced cook was once a complete beginner.

Start cooking, and you will wonder how you ever managed without it.
